TimeEntry.Title: Liz- Computer Scan
TimeEntry.Notes:
Liz Account – Suspicious Email Activity:
A few users reported receiving an email that appeared to be sent from Liz’s account. Liz confirmed that she did not send the email. As a precaution, we immediately reset her account password and informed Dave about the password change. Security scans were performed using CrowdStrike, Huntress, and Microsoft Defender, and all results came back clean. TimeEntry.Notes:
Title: Procedure – Fix DocuSign File Path Error in NewStar Sales Application

Initial Issue
When the user logs into the NewStar Sales application and selects DocuSign, the system displays an error stating that the files do not exist in the specified path.

Users/Staff Involved

  • Traci

  • Kris

  • Renan (BWH)

  • William (unable to access files)

Environment

  • Application: NewStar Sales

  • File Paths:

    • Production: C:\NSTAR\SalesP\splive\system32

    • Source: C:\NSTAR\SalesP\splive\Src\NSSLogin

    • Proto: C:\NSTAR\SalesP\spproto\Src\NSSLogin

Troubleshooting Steps Performed

  1. User Verification

    • Confirmed the user could log into the NewStar Sales application.

    • Issue occurred specifically when selecting DocuSign.

  2. RDP Session Check

    • Connected to the affected system using RDP.

    • Verified the environment and application mapping.

  3. Permission Verification

    • Checked folder permissions for the user account.

    • Verified access using:

      • Admin account

      • Traci's account

    • Confirmed permissions were not the root cause.

  4. File Path Validation

    • Error indicated that required files were missing fro

TimeEntry.Title: Pon- onedrive sync
TimeEntry.Notes:
Issue: OneDrive sync stopped at 72 files (4.1 GB remaining). The issue is not yet resolved.

Troubleshooting Steps Performed

  1. Restarted and Reset OneDrive

    • Quit OneDrive and re-linked the account.

    • Ran the reset command:

    C:\Program Files\Microsoft OneDrive\onedrive.exe /reset

  2. Checked OneDrive Sync Logs

    • Ran the following PowerShell command to view recent sync diagnostics:

    Get-Content "$env:LocalAppData\Microsoft\OneDrive\logs\Business1\SyncDiagnostics.log" -Tail 20

  3. Restarted OneDrive Process

    • Closed OneDrive completely using Task Manager.

  4. Cleared Office File Cache

    • Opened Run (Win + R) and navigated to:

    %localappdata%\Microsoft\Office\16.0\OfficeFileCache

    • Cleared the cache.

  5. Enabled Win32 Long Paths

    • Attempted to enable via Local Group Policy Editor:

      • Policy: Enable Win32 long paths

      • Status initially showed Not Configured.

    • Enabled long paths via Registry Editor:

      HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem

      Set:

      LongPathsEnabled = 1

  6. OneDrive Local Configuration

    • Located the configuration folder:

    %localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\settings

    • Folder contains directories such as Business1.

    • Note: The configuration folder was not deleted/cleared.

  7. Internet / TLS Settings Check

    • Opened Internet Options (inetcpl.cpl).

    • Verified that TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 are enabled.

    • Performed an Internet Settings Reset.

  8. Account Reconnection

    • Signed out of OneDrive and unlinked the PC, then signed back in.

  9. File Isolation Attempt

    • Identified an Excel file that was blocking the sync.

    • Moved the Excel file from the OneDrive folder to the C: drive.

Current Status

  • Sync is still stuck at 72 files (4.1 GB).

  • Issue remains unresolved and requires further investigation.

TimeEntry.Title: Pon- onedrive sync
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per received handover from previous shift, OneDrive was stuck in the "Looking for changes" state, preventing files from syncing.

Solution Steps:

  1. Signed Out and Unlinked OneDrive:

    • We started by signing out of OneDrive and unlinking the account from the PC through the OneDrive settings.

  2. Uninstalled OneDrive:

    • We uninstalled OneDrive from Settings > Apps > Apps & Features and also used PowerShell to remove any lingering files if the app couldn't be uninstalled normally.

  3. Removed OneDrive Files and Folders:

    • After unlinking OneDrive, we removed leftover OneDrive files from C:\Program Files\Microsoft OneDrive and other related folders, ensuring that no residual files were causing the issue.

  4. Cleared Registry Entries:

    • We checked the Windows registry for OneDrive-related entries (in HKEY_CURRENT_USER and HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE) and deleted them to ensure no hidden settings persisted.

  5. Rebooted the PC:

    • After making changes, we rebooted the system to apply all settings and ensure that no processes were interfering.

  6. Reinstalled OneDrive:

    • We downloaded the latest version of OneDrive and reinstalled it, then signed back in with the correct account to sync files.

  7. Checked Sync Settings:

    • We verified the sync settings within OneDrive and ensured that the file paths were correct and there were no syncing conflicts.

  8. Forced a Sync:

    • After reinstalling OneDrive, we forced a sync by right-clicking the OneDrive icon and selecting "Sync Now", which resolved the "Looking for changes" issue.

    • Created one test folder and it was showing synced successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: MWilliams issue regarding newstar sales
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

  • Issue: Document not generating after clicking on DocuSign in Newstar application.

  • Stakeholders Involved:

    • Newstar support team

    • Tracy

    • Rashad

    • Mike Williams

  • Resolution:

    • After connecting with the support team and stakeholders, the root cause was identified: the application was missing necessary drivers and was pointing to the wrong setup.

    • The application was reconfigured to point to the correct setup, and the missing drivers were installed.

  • Conclusion: The issue has been resolved, and the ticket can be closed.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: PC Performance Issues Due to Full C Drive – Upgrade Recommended
TimeEntry.Notes: Rick had issue with opening PDF.

TimeEntry.Title: Pon- onedrive sync
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue:
Rishad had previously fixed the OneDrive issue on the system, but after that, there was an issue with the scan-to-OneDrive functionality from the printer. The scan was not successfully uploaded to OneDrive due to a changed network path and permission issues.

Actions Taken:

  • Connected with Rishad to investigate the issue.

  • Identified that the network path for the scanner was changed after fixing the OneDrive issue.

  • Fixed the path and permissions to ensure the printer can correctly send scans to OneDrive.

  • Tested the scanning functionality after the changes, and confirmed it works as expected.

Resolution:
The scan-to-OneDrive functionality is now working fine, and the issue has been resolved. The ticket has been closed.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: PC Performance Issues Due to Full C Drive – Upgrade Recommended
TimeEntry.Notes: Visited Brandywine,  Loaner laptop (add domain). Add the Microsoft accounts, did the sync. logged into the application used by Rick. Started one-drive sync on the laptop. setup the laptop on his table. Pon had an issue with scanner. scanner folder was not syncing to onedrive folder. checked the permission. add AP scan on permissions for the folder, change the path on printer. tested. and worked. (Parveen assisted).

TimeEntry.Title: Installed Technijian tools on new Loaner system for rick
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the handover received from Rishad, the task was reviewed and work was carried out accordingly. The loaner machine was renamed to Loaner-03. All required applications have been installed and configured on the new workstation, including Zoom, Adobe Creative Cloud (logged in with rick@brandywine-homes.com, Project, InformXL Ribbon installer, Ring Central, and the NSE client. Outlook and Microsoft Teams have also been set up and verified for access. Files from the previous machine BWH-HQ-LT-06, including Desktop, Downloads, Documents, and other user folders, were successfully transferred to this PC, and OneDrive has been mapped. Browser data migration has also been completed, including passwords and bookmarks for both Chrome and Microsoft Edge. Printer setup could not be completed because the printers on the source machine BWH-HQ-LT-06 are currently showing offline, so they could not be identified or added to the Loaner-03 machine. The other remaining task is configuring Sophos VPN, which is currently pending and requires the user’s credentials to complete the setup. Updated the same to Rishad on Team chat.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: OneDrive
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a request from Jody Matye reporting that they were working remotely and were unable to connect to OneDrive.

Connected to the system remotely and performed troubleshooting to identify the cause of the OneDrive connectivity issue. During the session, the issue was reviewed and discussed with Jody through the remote chat while performing the necessary checks and corrective steps.

After completing the troubleshooting steps, the OneDrive connection was successfully restored and verified to be working properly. Confirmation was obtained from Jody that OneDrive was functioning normally.

Current Status:
Issue resolved. The system and OneDrive are working as expected. The user has been informed to contact the helpdesk if the issue occurs again.

TimeEntry.Notes: Got the alert for BWH on OPS manager that probe connection to the central server is down. SO connected to server, checked the probe service, it was stuck, so restart the probe service. Now the probe is up. After that when checked the devices for BWH, all devices were showing down. So troubleshoot the issue on devices and now sensors are up except the machines which are offline.

TimeEntry.Title: Interface 'Realtek PCIe GbE Family Controller-Ethernet' is shutdown & Disk Utilization
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWH-HQ-LT-09 giving alert Interface 'Realtek PCle Gbe Family Controller- Ethernet' is shutdown. So login into the portal and upon investigation, found that the interface was not in use. Delete the interface, rediscover the interface and added the used one from portal.and clean Temp files from diskcleanup. After completing these steps, all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Failed Veeam backup jobs for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the backups for BWH, cloud backups got failed. SO connected to the server and troubleshoot the issue. The issue with the expired certificate for the cloud backup job was resolved by updating the SSL certificate on the Veeam Backup and Replication server. The new certificate was imported into the appropriate certificate store on the server, ensuring it was correctly associated with the myveeamspc.technijian.com service provider. After restarting the necessary Veeam services, the repository was rescanned, and the backup job for the cloud repository successfully ran without any further certificate errors.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - BWH-HQ-VBR-01: (10.1.1.35) - Disk Utilization is 69%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to BWH-HQ-VBR-01, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 43% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: HELP - N: drive not showing on my laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Traci reported an issue accessing the N-drive from the home system. Connected to the system and confirmed that the VPN was successfully connected. Verified connectivity by pinging the application server via IP address, which was successful; however, the server name was not resolving. Reviewed network settings and firewall configuration, with no issues identified. Added an entry in the host file to resolve the application server name. After the update, the server name successfully resolved, and the N-drive was mapped. Traci is now able to access the N-drive without issues.

TimeEntry.Title: Please help me - Myle Phan with VPN log in
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a ticket via email regarding a VPN connectivity issue where the user was unable to establish an RDP session.

Connected to the system and verified that the VPN connection was successfully established; however, RDP access was not working. Tested RDP locally on the server, which was functioning correctly, indicating the issue was not with the RDP service itself.

Attempted RDP from another system and encountered the same issue. Restarted relevant services and rebooted the server, but the problem persisted. Verified RDP session settings and firewall rules, all of which were correctly configured.

Reinstalled the certificates on the server and tested again from the user’s system. RDP access was successfully restored, confirming the issue was related to certificate configuration.

Issue resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam Backup and Replication update to latest release on version 13.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into VBR server for BWH and then download the latest version on Veeam application. Take a snapshot of the VBR server before update and then run and complete the pending updates on the server. Once the application has been updated then start and verify all the services and rescan repositories. Start a backup job and complete that successfully.

TimeEntry.Notes:
Traci reported regarding the NewStar Application that the repoer status stuck in Queue....

Ticket is already open with the support. so had a word with lauren constellation support Agent...

Shared the remote session with her and checked the settings and services found that serives was stopped...

so restarted the service and ask traci to check now she confirmed its fine now..

so checked the Eventviewer logs however didn't found any error logs..

so informed traci that we are checking this and also make sure that after the maintenance window the service won't be affected and should be running....
